zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir William Hay, Laird of Locherworth and Yester於1375年出生在蘇格蘭米德洛迪安時，他的父親Sir Thomas De Haye, Sheriff Of Peebles21歲，母親Johanna Gifford29歲。他於大約1400年與Alice de la Haye結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和6個女兒的父母。他去世於1421年8月在英國蘇格蘭易亞斯特 洛迪安也斯特也斯特 卡素享年46歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭哈丁頓史爾也斯特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir William Hay, Laird of Locherworth and Yester于1375年出生在苏格兰米德洛迪安时，他的父亲Sir Thomas De Haye, Sheriff Of Peebles21岁，母亲Johanna Gifford29岁。他于大约1400年与Alice de la Haye结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和6个女儿的父母。他去世于1421年8月在英国苏格兰易亞斯特 洛迪安也斯特也斯特 卡素享年46岁，埋葬在苏格兰哈丁頓史爾也斯特。

nameMeanings.name: Hay,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish and English (northern; of Norman origin): habitational name from any of several places in Normandy called La Haie or La Haye (Old French haie ‘hedge, enclosure’, ‘forest for hunting deer and other animals’, a borrowing of the ancient Germanic word haga). Robert de Haia or de la Haye is known to have come from La Haye-du-Puits in Manche; he was the founder of Boxgrove Priory in Sussex (1123), and holder of the Honor of Halnaker (Sussex) and (by marriage) the barony of Kolswein (Lincolnshire). The Norman name was also taken to Ireland, where it has since flourished in the county of Wexford as Hay and Hayes . Elsewhere in Ireland the name usually has a native Irish origin, see below.

English: topographic name from Middle English hay(e), heye, heghe ‘enclosure’ (Old English (ge)hæg) or ‘forest fenced off for hunting’ (Old French haie); or else a habitational name from a place so called, such as Hay (in Herefordshire and Westmorland) or Hey in Scammonden (Yorkshire). It was no doubt sometimes synonymous with Hayward .
English: nickname for a tall man, from Middle English heigh, hey, high ‘high, tall’ (Old English hēah).

variants.name: High
meanings: English:
nickname for a tall man, from Middle English heigh, hey, high ‘high, tall’ (Old English hēah). Compare Hay 3.
topographic name for someone who lived ‘(on the) height or hill’, from Middle English heigh ‘height, hilltop’ (Old English hēah). Compare Hight .

variants.name: Hai
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Arabic/Muslim Abdul, Syed, Arifa, Atta, Faiz, Hamid, Iftekhar, Iskandar, Mohamed, Mohammad, Mohannad.  Vietnamese Mui, Pham, Tran, Vo, Bui, Cao, Chau, Dinh, Duong, Nguyen, Sau, Vo Van.  Jewish Ilan, Ronen, Shlomo.  Chinese Chan, Chiming, Lung, Wai Fun, Yin.
Muslim (mainly the Indian subcontinent): from a personal name based on Arabic ḥayy ‘alive’. Al-Ḥayy ‘the Living’ is an attribute of Allah.
Jewish: variant of   Hay  .

variants.name: Dehay
meanings: French: topographic name, with fused preposition de ‘from’, for someone who lived by an enclosure, from the masculine form of Old French haye ‘hedge’; or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word (see Hay 9).
Altered form (also De Hay) of Dutch De Haij or De Haaij, which is probably of French origin (see above).

variants.name: Haig
meanings: Scottish and Irish (of Norman origin): habitational name from any of various places in northern France named with Old Norse hagi ‘enclosure’, a word with cognates in most ancient Germanic languages. Compare Hay . The Haigs of Bemersyde in Berwickshire derive their name from the district of La Hague in Manche.
Scottish and Irish (Cavan): shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Thaoig and Mac Thaidhg (see McCaig ).

variants.name: Hayman
meanings: English: topographic name probably from Middle English hai(e), hei(e) ‘enclosure’ (Old English (ge)hæg), denoting a dweller by an enclosure (see Hay 2 ), or else a nickname for a ‘keeper of the enclosure’ (compare Hayward ).
English: nickname for a tall man, from Middle English heigh, high(e) (Old English hēah); see also Hay 3.
English: occupational name for a seller of hay, from Middle English hai, hei (Old English hēg, hīg).

variants.name: Hayes
meanings: Irish (Cork): shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hAodha ‘descendant of Aodh’, a personal name meaning ‘fire’. Compare McCoy . In some cases especially in County Wexford, the surname is of English origin (see below), having been taken to Ireland by the Normans.
English: variant of Hay , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

English: topographic name from the plural form of Middle English hay(e), heye, heghe ‘enclosure’ (see Hay ), sometimes used as a collective noun for a farm, especially in Devon, where it is a frequent minor placename. Compare Hain .

variants.name: Haycock
meanings: English: from a pet form of the Middle English personal name Hai + coc; see Hay 4 and compare Cocke .

variants.name: Haylett
meanings: English (Norfolk): from a pet form (double diminutive) of the personal name Hai (see Hay 4), from Hai + -el + -ot.

variants.name: Hey
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): variant of   Hay  .
Dutch: variant, mostly Americanized (or archaic) and Flemish, of Heij, a topographic name for someone who lived on a heath, Dutch hei, heide.
German: metonymic occupational name for a grower or mower of grass, from Middle High German höu ‘grass, hay’.

nameMeanings.name: Laird
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: status name for a landlord or landowner, from northern Middle English laverd ‘lord’.

variants.name: Lard
meanings: Scottish: altered form of   Laird  .

variants.name: Leard
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: variant of   Laird  .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
Chinese:
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: and
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of French Canadian Hénault or directly of French Hunault (see Henault 2).
Catalan (Añó): Castilianized form of Anyó, probably from the old personal name Annone.
Japanese: variant of   Anno  .

variants.name: Anno
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Japanese Masayuki, Tadashi, Yoshito.
Japanese: written 安野 ‘peaceful plain’. This surname is rare in Japan proper, and may have originated in the Ryūkyū Islands, where it is pronounced   Ano  .

variants.name: Henault
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Andre, Emile, Lucien, Martial, Pierre, Camille, Fernand, Laurier, Monique, Normand, Philippe.
French (Hénault): from the ancient Germanic personal name Haginwald, composed of the elements hagin ‘enclosure, protected place’ and wald ‘power, authority’.
Altered form (Hénault) of French Hunault: from the ancient Germanic personal name Hunwald, composed of the elements hun ‘bear cub’ and wald ‘power, authority’. Compare Ano and Eno .

nameMeanings.name: Yester
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Origin undetermined.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir William Hay, Laird of Locherworth and Yester was born in 1375, in Midlothian, Scotland, his father, Sir Thomas De Haye, Sheriff Of Peebles, was 21 and his mother, Johanna Gifford, was 29. He married Alice de la Haye about 1400. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 6 daughters. He died in August 1421, in Yester Castle, Yester, East Lothian, Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 46, and was buried in Yester, Haddingtonshire, Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir William Hay, Laird of Locherworth and Yester est né en 1375, à Midlothian, Écosse, son père, Sir Thomas De Haye, Sheriff Of Peebles, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Johanna Gifford, avait 29 ans. Il a épousé Alice de la Haye environ 1400. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 6 filles. Il est décédé en août 1421, à Yester Castle, Yester, East Lothian, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 46 ans, et a été enterré à Yester, Haddingtonshire, Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir William Hay, Laird of Locherworth and Yester 1375 in Midlothian, Scotland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Thomas De Haye, Sheriff Of Peebles 21 und seine Mutter Johanna Gifford 29. Er hat um 1400 Alice de la Haye geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 6 Töchter. Er starb im August 1421 im Alter von 46 Jahren in Yester Castle, Yester, East Lothian, Schottland, United Kingdom und wurde in Yester, Haddingtonshire, Scotland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William Hay, Laird of Locherworth and Yester è nato il nel 1375 nel Midlothian, Scotland, il suo padre, Sir Thomas De Haye, Sheriff Of Peebles, aveva 21 anni e il suo madre, Johanna Gifford, aveva 29 anni. Lui sposato Alice de la Haye circa 1400. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 6 figlie. Morì nel agosto 1421, nel Yester Castle, Yester, East Lothian, Scozia, Regno Unito, all'età di 46 anni, e fu sepolto a Yester, Haddingtonshire, Scotland.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir William Hay, Laird of Locherworth and Yesterが1375年にスコットランドミドロスアンで生まれた時，父Sir Thomas De Haye, Sheriff Of Peeblesは21歳，母Johanna Giffordは29歳であった。約1400年にAlice de la Hayeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と6人の娘の親であった。彼は1421年8月にイギリススコットランドエアスト ロスアンイエスターイエスター カストルで46歳で死去し，スコットランドハッドイントンシュアイアイエスターにて埋葬された。

ko.Gifford Yester Haddingtonshire Scotland: 영국 스코틀랜드 이스트 로스이언 예스터 지퍼드
ko.Yester Castle, Gifford, East Lothian, Sc: 영국 스코틀랜드 이스트 로스이언 예스터 예스터 카스틀
ko.Yester Castle, Yester, Haddingtonshire,: 영국 스코틀랜드 이스트 로스이언 예스터 예스터 카스틀
ko.lifeSummary: Sir William Hay, Laird of Locherworth and Yester(이)가 1375년 스컷랜드 미들로스이언에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Thomas De Haye, Sheriff Of Peebles(은)는 21세였고, 그의 어머니 Johanna Gifford(은)는 29세였다. 그는 대략 1400년 Alice de la Haye(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1421년 8월 영국 스코틀랜드 이스트 로스이언 예스터 예스터 카스틀에서 46세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드 하딩토실르 예스터에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William Hay, Laird of Locherworth and Yester nasceu em 1375, em Midlothian, Scotland, seu pai, Sir Thomas De Haye, Sheriff Of Peebles, tinha 21 anos e sua mãe, Johanna Gifford, tinha 29 anos. Ele casou-se com Alice de la Haye aproximadamente 1400. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 6 filhas. Ele faleceu em agosto de 1421, em Yester Castle, Yester, East Lothian, Escócia, United Kingdom, com 46 anos, e foi sepultado em Yester, Haddingtonshire, Scotland.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir William Hay, Laird of Locherworth and Yester родился в 1375 г. в местности мидлотян, Шотландия, его отцу по имени Sir Thomas De Haye, Sheriff Of Peebles было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Johanna Gifford, 29. Он женился на женщине по имени Alice de la Haye около 1400 г. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 6 дочерей. Он умер август 1421 г. в местности иестир кастл, иестир, эаст лотян, Шотландия, Великобритания в возрасте 46 лет и был похоронен в местности иестир, хаддингтоншир, Шотландия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir William Hay, Laird of Locherworth and Yester nació en 1375, en Midlothian, Scotland, su papá, Sir Thomas De Haye, Sheriff Of Peebles, tenía 21 años y su mamá, Johanna Gifford, tenía 29 años. Se casó con Alice de la Haye aproximadamente 1400. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 6 hijas. Murió en agosto de 1421, en Yester Castle, Yester, East Lothian, Escocia, Reino Unido, a la edad de 46 años, y fue sepultado en Yester, Haddingtonshire, Scotland.
